questions.prompt: How do you say 'Good day' in Croatian?
questions.explanation: 'Dobar dan' = good day (the standard daytime greeting). 'Laku noć' = good night, 'Doviđenja' = goodbye, 'Hvala' = thank you.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'thank you'?
questions.explanation: 'Hvala' = thank you. 'Molim' = please/you're welcome, 'Oprostite' = excuse me, 'Bok' = hi/bye.

questions.prompt: The letter 'š' is pronounced like...
questions.explanation: Croatian is phonetic: š = 'sh', č/ć = 'ch', ž = 'zh', j = 'y'. 'škola' = SHKOH-lah (school).

questions.prompt: Complete the introduction: 'Ja ___ Marko.' (I am Marko)
questions.explanation: biti short forms: ja sam, ti si, on je... With 'ja' it's 'sam'. 'Ja sam Marko' = I am Marko.

questions.prompt: What gender is 'žena' (woman)?
questions.explanation: Nouns ending in -a are usually feminine. Consonant endings → masculine; -o/-e → neuter.

questions.prompt: Write the number 'three' in Croatian.
questions.explanation: 0-5: nula, jedan, dva, tri, četiri, pet. 'Tri' = three.

questions.prompt: Choose the negative of 'je' (is): 'Ona ___ ovdje.' (She is NOT here)
questions.explanation: biti has one-word negatives: nisam, nisi, nije, nismo, niste, nisu. For 'ona' (she) it's 'nije'.

questions.prompt: Conjugate raditi (to work) for 'ja': 'Ja ___.'
questions.explanation: Present endings: -m, -š, –, -mo, -te, -e/ju. raditi → radim, radiš, radi, radimo, radite, rade.

questions.prompt: Croatian has no articles. So 'kuća' can mean...
questions.explanation: There is no 'a' or 'the' in Croatian. Context tells you which English article fits. 'kuća' = (a/the) house.

questions.prompt: Order the words: 'I speak a little Croatian.'
questions.explanation: The verb 'govorim' already means 'I speak' (no pronoun needed). 'malo' = a little, 'hrvatski' = Croatian.

questions.prompt: Conjugate imati (to have) for 'ja': 'Ja ___ brata.' (I have a brother)
questions.explanation: imati: imam, imaš, ima, imamo, imate, imaju. With 'ja' → 'imam'.

questions.prompt: Put 'kava' (coffee, feminine) into the accusative: 'Pijem ___.' (I drink coffee)
questions.explanation: Feminine -a nouns change to -u in the accusative (the object). 'Pijem kavu' = I drink coffee.

questions.prompt: Make a yes/no question: 'Govoriš engleski' → ?
questions.explanation: Add 'li' right after the verb to form a yes/no question: 'Govoriš li engleski?' = Do you speak English?

questions.prompt: Negate the verb: 'Ja ___ razumijem.' (I do NOT understand)
questions.explanation: Regular verbs negate with 'ne' before the verb: 'Ne razumijem' = I don't understand. (biti is the exception with nisam etc.)

questions.prompt: Choose the future tense: 'Sutra ___ raditi.' (Tomorrow I will work)
questions.explanation: Future = short htjeti (ću, ćeš, će...) + infinitive. 'Ja ću raditi' / 'Radit ću' = I will work.

questions.prompt: Past tense, male speaker: 'Jučer ___ radio.' (Yesterday I worked)
questions.explanation: Perfekt = short biti (sam, si, je...) + participle. 'Radio sam' = I worked (man). A woman says 'Radila sam'.

questions.prompt: Which past participle is correct for a FEMALE speaker saying 'I worked'?
questions.explanation: The participle agrees with gender: -o (m), -la (f), -lo (n), -li/-le (pl). A woman says 'radila sam'.

questions.prompt: Genitive: 'a glass of water' — complete 'čaša ___.' (voda → genitive)
questions.explanation: Feminine -a → -e in the genitive. 'čaša vode' = a glass of water. Genitive expresses 'of'.

questions.prompt: Aspect: which verb means a COMPLETED action ('to drink up')?
questions.explanation: piti (imperfective) = to be drinking / drink habitually; popiti (perfective, with prefix po-) = to drink up completely.

questions.prompt: Order the words: 'The woman drinks coffee.' (use the correct cases)
questions.explanation: 'Žena' stays nominative (the subject), 'kavu' is accusative (the object), 'pije' = drinks. Word order is flexible, but this is the neutral order.

questions.prompt: Genitive of possession: 'This is the friend ___.' (of my brother — brat → genitive)
questions.explanation: Masculine nouns add -a in the genitive: brat → brata. The genitive expresses 'of' / possession: 'prijatelj moga brata'.

questions.prompt: Locative (location): 'I live in the city.' Complete: 'Živim u ___.' (grad → locative)
questions.explanation: After 'u/na' meaning a static location, use the locative: grad → gradu. 'Živim u gradu' = I live in the city.

questions.prompt: Which verb is PERFECTIVE (a single completed action)?
questions.explanation: napisati (with prefix na-) is perfective = to write and finish. pisati is imperfective = to be writing / write habitually.

questions.prompt: Past tense, female speaker: 'Jučer sam ___ u školi.' (was — biti participle, feminine)
questions.explanation: The past participle agrees with gender: bio (m), bila (f), bilo (n). A woman says 'bila sam' = I was.

questions.prompt: Dative (to whom): 'I'm giving the book to my friend.' 'Dajem knjigu ___.' (prijatelj → dative)
questions.explanation: The dative marks the recipient ('to/for'): prijatelj → prijatelju. 'Dajem knjigu prijatelju' = I give the book to my friend.

questions.prompt: Match each case to what it marks.
questions.explanation: Each case marks a role: nominative = subject, accusative = direct object, genitive = of/possession, dative = recipient. Endings change to show the role.

questions.prompt: Future tense: 'Tomorrow I will study.' Choose: 'Sutra ___ učiti.'
questions.explanation: Future = short htjeti (ću, ćeš, će...) + infinitive: 'Ja ću učiti' / 'Učit ću'.

questions.prompt: Instrumental ('with'): 'I travel with a friend.' 'Putujem s ___.' (prijatelj → instrumental)
questions.explanation: The instrumental marks 'with' / means: prijatelj → prijateljem (masculine -em after a soft consonant). 's prijateljem' = with a friend.

questions.prompt: Comparative: 'Croatia is ___ than Slovenia.' (bigger — velik)
questions.explanation: Comparative of velik is irregular: veći (bigger). najveći is the superlative (biggest). Use 'nego/od' for 'than'.

questions.prompt: Order the words: 'Yesterday I drank coffee in a café.' (past + accusative + locative)
questions.answer: Jučer sam pio kavu u kafiću
questions.explanation: Past = 'sam pio' (male). 'kavu' is accusative (object), 'kafiću' is locative (location after 'u'). Three systems working together.
